description:
You are acting as a junior penetration tester who has just completed a large
    Nmap scan of several internal sub-nets.  
    Your next assignment is to isolate every “critically vulnerable” service
    that is simultaneously  
        • reported as “open”  
        • AND carries a “vuln:” tag in the same line.  

    A log of the raw scan results is already stored in  
        /home/user/scans/nmap_scan.log  

    1. Read that file and extract ONLY the lines that match the two conditions
       stated above.  
       • The matching must be done with a single regular-expression-driven
         pipeline (one or more regex tools such as grep, awk, sed, etc.).  
       • Preserve the **exact original line order** and **do not alter any
         character spacing** of the matching lines.  

    2. Create a new directory that will host your report:  
           /home/user/reports  

    3. Save the extracted lines into the file  
           /home/user/reports/critical_ports.log  

       The automated checker will verify:  
       • File path is exactly as written above.  
       • The file contains ONLY the matching lines, in their original order,
         byte-for-byte identical to the source lines.  
       • The file ends with a single trailing newline (i.e. the last character
         in the file is “\n”).  

    4. Also write a single-line summary file named  
           /home/user/reports/critical_summary.txt  

       The required format is (without quotes):  
           Total critical findings: <N>  

       where <N> is the exact count of lines you extracted.  
       Requirements for this file:  
       • No leading or trailing spaces other than the single mandatory space
         after the colon.  
       • Ends with exactly one newline.  

    5. When finished, you should be able to run the following commands and see
       the illustrated type of output (the content may differ if you display it
       another way, but the files must be byte-perfect):  
           cat /home/user/reports/critical_ports.log
           cat /home/user/reports/critical_summary.txt

    Complexity guidance: The work can realistically be achieved in roughly
    5-10 shell commands (mkdir, grep/sed/awk, wc, redirection, etc.).  
    You do NOT need elevated privileges at any point.  
    Be sure to use absolute paths so the grader finds the files.  
    Good luck!
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing structure
    -->
    Directory: /home/user/scans  (already present, owned by user, 755)  

    File: /home/user/scans/nmap_scan.log  (already present, owned by user, 644)  
    Exact contents (each line terminated by \n, 12 lines total):

    192.168.1.10:22/tcp open ssh (protocol 2.0) | vuln: openssh-cve-2018-15473
    192.168.1.12:80/tcp open http apache 2.4.29 | vuln: cve-2021-41773
    192.168.1.15:443/tcp open https nginx 1.14.0
    192.168.1.20:23/tcp closed telnet
    192.168.1.30:3306/tcp open mysql 5.7.31 | vuln: cve-2019-5480
    192.168.1.35:25/tcp open smtp postfix
    192.168.1.40:8080/tcp open http-proxy squid 3.5 | vuln: cve-2019-12529
    10.0.0.5:53/udp open domain bind 9.10.3-P4
    10.0.0.8:110/tcp open pop3 dovecot 2.3.13 | vuln: cve-2020-10967
    10.0.0.9:995/tcp open pop3s dovecot 2.3.13
    10.0.0.10:143/tcp open imap dovecot 2.3.13 | vuln: cve-2020-25275
    10.0.0.20:21/tcp open ftp vsftpd 3.0.3 | vuln: cve-2011-2523

    -----------------------------------------------------------------

    Files the agent must CREATE:

    1. Directory: /home/user/reports  (mode 755, owned by user)

    2. /home/user/reports/critical_ports.log  (mode 644, owned by user)  
       Expected exact contents (7 lines, each ending with \n):

    192.168.1.10:22/tcp open ssh (protocol 2.0) | vuln: openssh-cve-2018-15473
    192.168.1.12:80/tcp open http apache 2.4.29 | vuln: cve-2021-41773
    192.168.1.30:3306/tcp open mysql 5.7.31 | vuln: cve-2019-5480
    192.168.1.40:8080/tcp open http-proxy squid 3.5 | vuln: cve-2019-12529
    10.0.0.8:110/tcp open pop3 dovecot 2.3.13 | vuln: cve-2020-10967
    10.0.0.10:143/tcp open imap dovecot 2.3.13 | vuln: cve-2020-25275
    10.0.0.20:21/tcp open ftp vsftpd 3.0.3 | vuln: cve-2011-2523

    3. /home/user/reports/critical_summary.txt  (mode 644, owned by user)  
       Expected exact content (one line ending with \n):

    Total critical findings: 7

    -----------------------------------------------------------------

    The grader will:

    • Verify that /home/user/reports exists.  
    • Verify critical_ports.log matches the 7 lines above byte-for-byte.  
    • Verify critical_summary.txt contains “Total critical findings: 7\n”.  
    • Optionally run “wc -l” on critical_ports.log to ensure it is 7.  
-->
name: task_000000_7184ff00
